# BDNF

## What This Gene Does

BDNF is the primary growth factor for synaptic plasticity and neurogenesis.

## Example Genotype

| SNP | Genotype | Significance | Evidence |
|-----|----------|-------------|----------|
| rs6265 | C;C (Val/Val) | Normal activity-dependent BDNF secretion | E1 |

## Health Relevance

### Neuroplasticity
Val/Val removes Met-related secretion defect. [priority:: high] [context:: research]

### Exercise Response
HIIT increases serum BDNF 2-3x; chronic exercise increases hippocampal volume.

## Drug Interactions

| Drug | Interaction | Mechanism |
|------|------------|-----------|
| Ketamine | Optimal responder | BDNF-TrkB fully functional |
| Sertraline | Normal response | BDNF supports SSRI neuroplasticity |

## Gene-Gene Interactions

- [[FKBP5]]: Stress persistence + rewiring capacity
- [[DRD2]]: D2 upregulation through exercise

## What Changes This

- Exercise (primary BDNF activator): HIIT 2-3x/week
- Learning/skill acquisition: Piano, language, skateboarding
- CBT/exposure therapy: Fear extinction is BDNF-dependent
- Sleep: BDNF consolidation in slow-wave sleep

## Confidence & Caveats

E1 evidence. Val/Val is common genotype (65-80% EUR). Provides capacity not outcomes.

> See [[Genetic Determinism - Limits and Caveats]]

## Sources

1. Egan MF et al. (2003) The BDNF val66met polymorphism affects activity-dependent secretion of BDNF. Cell 112:257-269.
